market_notes: April 20, 2026: Markets pulling back from record highs as Nasdaq snaps longest win streak since 1992 on renewed Iran/Hormuz tensions (oil jumping). IBD: 'Nasdaq Snaps Win Streak On Rising U.S.-Iran Tensions.' AAPL: Tim Cook stepping down, succeeded by John Ternus — major leadership transition creating near-term uncertainty for $4T market cap company. Semiconductor froth warning: Yahoo Finance 'Semiconductor stocks haven't been this hot since the dot-com bubble.' MRVL surging on Barrons 'How Google Is Playing Semiconductor Kingmaker—and Making Marvell a Winner' — exceptional catalyst for our +48% position. Portfolio at +6.1% total P&L with 12 positions, ~45% cash after exiting NEM and adding SNPS. Dense earnings week: April 21 (UNH, GE, NOC, RTX, HAL, EQT, ISRG), April 22 (TSLA, VRT, GEV, LRCX, BSX, NOW), April 23 (NEM, AXP, BX, FCX, INTC, LMT, NDAQ, NEE, TMO), April 28-30 (GOOG, META, MSFT, AMZN, NVDA, KLAC, AAPL mega-cap week).
watching: TSLA April 22 earnings — not held; +11.4% 1W into print; EV demand, robotaxi/FSD, SpaceX commentary are key read-throughs
VRT April 22 earnings — not held; key AI data center cooling infrastructure read-through for broader AI capex thesis; +4.8% 1W
GEV April 22 earnings — not held; grid/power infrastructure; Iran re-escalation makes energy/power demand thesis more complex

MRVL at -1.2% from 52W high with +48% unrealized — Barrons 'Google Playing Semiconductor Kingmaker' is fresh bullish catalyst; watching for profit-taking trigger or continuation signal; consider trimming if volume-driven distribution appears

AAPL Tim Cook departure — CEO succession to John Ternus creates near-term uncertainty; watching stock reaction and whether this creates a buying opportunity or selling pressure ahead of April 30 earnings
Semiconductor froth warning — Yahoo Finance comparing to dot-com bubble; watching for distribution signals in MRVL/AMD/NVDA/AVGO if rotation out of semis begins
Iran/Hormuz re-escalation — oil jumping again; watching for impact on broader equity market and any defense/energy rotation; RTX/LMT earnings April 21-23 will provide read-through
NOW +12% 1W despite covering at +0.8% — software sector strongly recovering; watching if this is sustained or dead-cat bounce into earnings April 22
SNPS new position — watching for continuation above entry and NASA/AI chip design demand confirmation
Mega-cap earnings April 28-30 (GOOG, META, MSFT, AMZN, NVDA, KLAC, AAPL) — AI capex commentary will be the dominant market driver; holding semiconductor positions into these prints
DDOG +17.9% 1W — software recovery play; watching for entry opportunity if pullback occurs

recent_runs.reasoning: **Macro:** Market regime is risk-on with benchmarks surging: SPY +3.9% 1W, QQQ +4.6% 1W, SMH +5.8% 1W — a strong week driven by Iran cease-fire hopes (Trump offering 'olive branch' per IBD), strong jobs data, and broad AI infrastructure momentum. Oil at $110 with Strait of Hormuz closure fears creates cross-current, but equity markets are rallying on diplomatic breakthrough hopes. Bond yields rising (strong jobs pushes back Fed cuts) remains a headwind for rate-sensitive growth but not derailing the AI/semi rally. **Sectors:** Semiconductors leading (SMH +5.8% 1W) — MRVL +15.4% 1W is extraordinary, INTC +17.7% 1W on Apollo deal, AMD +9.0% 1W, NVDA +6.0% 1W. Memory storage exploding: SNDK +17.7% 1W, WDC +10.5% 1W. Optical networking (LITE +9.9%, CIEN +8.1%) continuing higher. Defense strong: RTX +4.6%, LMT +3.6%. Gold (NEM +10.5%) remains dual-tailwind play. Banks rallying into earnings: GS +7.9% 1W, C +9.3% 1W, JPM +4.5% 1W. Energy lagging (CVX -5.8%, XOM -4.5%, LNG -4.3%) as Iran cease-fire hopes reduce oil premium. **Positions:** Portfolio performing well — MRVL +9.7% unrealized is the star, TSEM +10.6%, MU +2.7%, SNDK +3.3%, ARM +3.2%, NVDA +1.9%. At risk: LNG -4.3% 1W as Iran de-escalation thesis hurts energy; NEM -0.8% unrealized but still +10.5% 1W strong. ASML flat to slightly negative with earnings April 15 and China export bill headwind. GS EARNINGS ALERT: GS reports April 13 (today/tomorrow). Holding 4 shares at +0.7% unrealized. BofA says banks set for in-line to better quarter — capital markets reactivation thesis intact, GS upgraded Netflix to Buy this week showing active dealmaking. Will HOLD into earnings — small 4-share position (~3.4% weight) limits binary risk, thesis intact on capital markets rebound. Key decisions: (1) LNG is now a problem — Iran cease-fire hopes are directly undermining the LNG war premium thesis, stock -4.3% 1W while energy sector broadly weak; CLOSE LNG. (2) TSEM at +10.6% unrealized and 39.7x fwd P/E with only +14% revenue growth — valuation stretched, GlobalFoundries patent suit risk, stock +10.6% 1W; TRIM to take profits. (3) MRVL is exceptional — +15.4% 1W, +44.7% 1M, new Buy initiation from Insider Monkey, NVIDIA $2B deal thesis, fwd P/E 20.3x on 22% growth is reasonable; HOLD and consider adding. (4) WDC +10.5% 1W — same AI storage thesis as SNDK; could add WDC as complement. (5) MPWR +12.3% 1W, Citi bullish, fwd P/E 45.7x on 21% growth — interesting but expensive. (6) AMD +9.0% 1W with Citi 30-day positive catalyst watch, fwd P/E 20.4x on 34% growth — compelling for swing trade. (7) UNH +8.6% 1W on Medicare rate boost — fwd P/E 14x on 12% growth with fresh FDA catalyst; interesting defensive/health play. (8) C +9.3% 1W reports April 14 — could add small pre-earnings position given BofA 'in-line to better quarter' call. (9) ASML earnings April 15 — holding 5 shares, small position; China export bill is headwind but Cohen buying bullish; HOLD small position. Actions: SELL LNG (thesis broken by Iran cease-fire), TRIM TSEM (take profits on 10.6% gain at stretched valuation), BUY AMD (Citi catalyst watch + strong momentum + reasonable valuation), BUY WDC (AI storage supercycle complement to SNDK, +10.5% 1W, fwd P/E 22.3x). **Cash:** Reducing cash from ~61% toward ~50% by deploying into high-conviction setups post-Iran de-escalation signal; maintaining dry powder for post-earnings deployment this week (JPM/C/JNJ April 14, ASML/MS/PNC April 15, TSM/NFLX April 16).

The cease-fire removes the geopolitical premium from energy/defense and accelerates AI infrastructure rotation. **Sectors:** Semiconductors in full breakout (SMH +10.3% 1W) — MU +20.4% 1W, SNDK +22.9% 1W, WDC +25.2% 1W, MRVL +15.6% 1W all explosive. (3) BUY ANET — AI networking infrastructure, +18.1% 1W, 1.87x vol ratio showing institutional accumulation, fwd P/E 33.9x on 29% revenue growth reasonable, 52W high -12% recovery potential. (4) BUY AVGO — BofA names as top AI chip driver, +13.3% 1W, fwd P/E 19.7x on 30% revenue growth, near 52W highs; strong conviction add.
